question.label: Self-read opener
question.name: Before I share my view, how do you think the last stretch has gone?
question.description: Anchors the conversation in their self-assessment first; gap vs manager's view becomes a signal.

answer: I can name wins, but I am not always sure they match the team bar.
note: He can identify wins but explicitly says he is unsure whether they meet the team bar, which is a strong clarity gap with mild engagement still present.

question.label: Thread follow
question.name: name wins, always — can you say more about what that means for you right now?
question.description: Runtime thread-follow injected because planner did not follow substantive answer.

answer: Feedback has helped, though I still miss consistency under time pressure.
note: He says feedback is helping but names a specific recurring miss—consistency under time pressure—which confirms a clear standards gap while staying engaged with improving it.

question.label: Time pressure misses
question.name: Under time pressure, what specifically starts to slip in your work?
question.description: Drills into his stated consistency issue so the quality gap is named in observable terms.

answer: I sometimes focus on polish and lose sight of the core message.
note: He names a concrete clarity failure: under pressure he optimizes polish over the core message, which strongly signals an unclear quality hierarchy.

question.label: Concrete examples
question.name: Which recent piece of work best shows your current quality bar?
question.description: Forces a specific artifact into view so the feedback can be grounded in real work rather than abstractions.

answer: I want clearer examples of what excellent junior-level work looks like here.
note: Deficiency-as-request: he names missing examples of excellent junior-level work, signalling strong role-bar confusion and mild growth blockage.

question.label: Thread follow
question.name: want clearer examples — can you say more about what that means for you right now?
question.description: Runtime thread-follow injected because planner did not follow substantive answer.

answer: When reviews are broad, I am not sure what to change first.
note: He names a concrete prioritisation problem in reviews — broad feedback leaves him unclear on what to change first, with a mild knock-on growth and capacity signal.

question.label: Thread follow
question.name: reviews broad, sure — can you say more about what that means for you right now?
question.description: Runtime thread-follow injected because planner did not follow substantive answer.

answer: I would like to grow faster in concept rationale, not just execution speed.
note: He names a specific growth direction in concept rationale, which is positive for growth but still signals some clarity gap about what development should focus on.

question.label: Quality misses
question.name: Where do you think your work most often misses the bar?
question.description: Names the recurring quality gap in a way that can lead to a concrete calibration discussion.

answer: A tighter loop on one skill at a time would help me improve.
note: Names a missing feedback structure — a tighter loop on one skill at a time — which is a deficiency-as-request signaling strong clarity gap and mild growth blockage.

question.label: Working definition
question.name: How do you decide when a design is ready to ship?
question.description: Reveals his internal threshold for done and whether it matches the team's expectation.

answer: If we align on concrete criteria, I can work against them confidently.
note: He says he needs aligned concrete criteria before he can judge readiness, which is a strong current clarity gap.

question.label: Next two weeks
question.name: What would you like to have changed by our next check-in?
question.description: Closes on a specific commitment for the next cycle without opening a new topic.

answer: I will start by writing one explicit priority rule I will use when time pressure hits this month.
note: Names a specific behavioural change for the next month — a clear positive growth commitment.
